summary: The main claim is: TLDR: “Key Phenomena in AI Risk” is an 8-week-long, facilitated reading group.
The main claim is: Policymakers should robustly regulate AI systems capable of deception.
Risks of AI Deception --------------------- After our survey of deceptive AI systems, we turn to considering the risks associated with AI systems.
The main claim is: If we want more people in the world who understand AGI safety, we need a place where it's accepted and encouraged to ask about the basics.
This is a safe space for people to ask stupid questions, so be kind!
The main claim is: As the title says, Meta trained 4 foundational models with 7B, 13B, 33B, and 65B parameters respectively, and is open sourcing them for research.
On downstream benchmarks, the models do comparably well with Chinchilla and PaLM and only a bit worse than Flan-PaLM-540B and code-davinci-002 / text-davinci-002 .
The main claim is: Hence when α is small we want to have more subspaces, each with fewer vectors but larger cosine similarities.
The main claim is: (I think Carl Shulman came up with the “hacking the SSH server” example, thanks to him for that.

After a month the AI is powerful enough that it decides it’s worth it to take overtly aggressive actions to ensure the physical security of its data centers and allow it to build lots of factories; this is probably the point at which we di…
The main claim is: “AGI safety” (Post 1) is about what the AGI actually does , not what it’s trying to do.
That said, “solving AGI alignment” is not exactly the same as “solving AGI safety”.

The main claim is: I think chess has some unique properties that make the results of training transformers on games and moves much more interpretable than doing the same on language alone or language and images.
This means a multi-modal chess language model could be investigated for symbol grounding on several levels of complexity.
The main claim is: A significant consideration is whether there will be a discontinuity in AI capabilities around HLMI .
Such a discontinuity could be from a jump in capabilities to HLMI or a jump from HLMI to significantly higher capabilities (or both).
The main claim is: For example, ofer wrote: I think using a well-chosen reward distribution is necessary, otherwise POWER depends on arbitrary choices in the design of the MDP's state graph.
In Pac-Man, the MDP model is uniquely defined by how we encode states and actions, and the part of the real world which our agent interfaces with.
The main claim is: As a result, we would like to use design for our artifacts rather than search.
Thus, predictive power alone is not sufficient to define human values.
